title: NOC 85103 – Nursery and greenhouse labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 85103 represents the specialized frontline workforce of Canada’s rapidly advancing "Indoor Farming" sector. As a TEER 5 occupation, these labourers are responsible for planting, cultivating, and harvesting everything from ornamental flowers and shrubs to greenhouse-grown vegetables and cannabis. In 2026, as Canada scales its high-tech greenhouse acreage to lead North America in year-round food security, these workers have become essential to the national economy. For immigration, this role is a high-priority category for the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway and the OINP In-Demand Skills stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level5Major Group85 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersSub-Major Group851 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersMinor Group8510 – Agriculture, horticulture and harvesting labourersNOC Code85103

duties: Propagation: Plant bulbs, seeds, and cuttings; perform grafting and budding of plants.
Cultivation: Transplant seedlings and rooted cuttings into larger containers or outdoor fields.
Monitoring: Regularly check plants for healthy growth and identify potential weed, insect, or disease problems.
Maintenance: Position and regulate sophisticated greenhouse irrigation and climate-control systems.
Chemical Application: Spray trees, shrubs, and flowers to prevent pests and diseases (often requiring specialized safety gear).
Customer Service: Provide advice to customers at garden centers regarding plant care and tool usage.
Operations: Operate tractors, forklifts, and specialized horticulture machinery for fertilizing and harvesting.
employment_requirements: No formal education is strictly required, though some secondary school is often preferred.
On-the-Job Training: This is the most common form of training provided by employers.
Certifications:A provincial Pest Applicators Certificate is required for workers who apply chemical fertilizers or pesticides.First Aid and WHMIS are standard safety requirements.
A provincial Pest Applicators Certificate is required for workers who apply chemical fertilizers or pesticides.
First Aid and WHMIS are standard safety requirements.
Physical stamina and the ability to perform repetitive motions in warm, humid environments are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Specialized roles (e.g., in cannabis or technical propagation) often command higher hourly wages closer to the "High" range.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 13.2% for the Canadian indoor farming market starting in 2026. As "National Food Self-Sufficiency" becomes a central policy goal, investments in high-tech glasshouses are scaling rapidly, creating thousands of new permanent and seasonal positions for international workers.
labour_market_demand: The demand for nursery and greenhouse labourers is Very Good and increasingly year-round. In 2026, Ontario (Leamington/Niagara) and British Columbia are the primary hubs, with Ontario standing as the largest greenhouse market in the country. The sector is currently experiencing a "Technological Shift," where demand is rising for workers who can operate in climate-controlled environments and assist with newly integrated robotics for harvesting and picking.
High Growth Sector: The Canadian indoor farming market is projected to reach over $11 billion by 2033, ensuring long-term job stability.
Controlled Environments: Unlike traditional field work, many greenhouse roles offer stable, climate-controlled working conditions regardless of the Canadian winter.
Specialization Opportunities: Gain experience in niche areas like Hydroponics, Cannabis cultivation, or Automated Irrigation Systems.
Priority Immigration Status: Included in the "Priority Processing" lists for 2026 work permits to address critical labor shortages.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time measure for workers already in Canada, targeting 33,000 applicants in essential sectors like agriculture.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): A direct PR route for workers in specific agricultural roles with a job offer in Ontario.
Sector-Specific Stream for Agriculture: A new IRCC initiative launching in 2026 to streamline PR for foreign workers in the greenhouse and fish processing sectors.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): Accessible for nursery workers with a job offer from designated employers in Atlantic Canada.
